full_response:
For any two vector fields on a (pseudo-)Riemannian manifold, say  

• V^a(x)  – the vector we want to “differentiate,” and  
• W^a(x)  – the vector that specifies the direction,

the directional (covariant) derivative of V^a in the direction of W^a is obtained by contracting W^a with the covariant derivative operator ∇_a:

D_W V^b ≡ W^a ∇_a V^b.          (1)

In a coordinate basis this becomes, using the definition ∇_a V^b = ∂_a V^b + Γ^b_{ac} V^c,

D_W V^b = W^a(∂_a V^b + Γ^b_{ac} V^c).          (2)

Equation (2) gives the explicit component form in terms of the Christoffel symbols Γ^b_{ac}.

\boxed{\,\displaystyle \text{Directional derivative of }V^{b}\text{ along }W^{a}: \quad D_{W}V^{b}=W^{a}\nabla_{a}V^{b}=W^{a}\bigl(\partial_{a}V^{b}+\Gamma^{b}_{\;ac}V^{c}\bigr)\,}

Confidence: 91%
